text.title: Lessons Learned Capture Process
text.summary: Rules for capturing lessons continuously with mandatory structured fields, 5-whys root cause, and session facilitation ground rules.

rule.statement: Reject any lesson submission that is missing a field or contains "TBD". Required: title (action-oriented, under 80 chars), project, date, category, impact_level with quantified effect, situation (facts only), root_cause (5-whys), lesson (generalizable, under 2 sentences), recommendation (action verb + owner_role + where_applied), evidence (artifact links).
rule.rationale: Free-text lessons degrade to noise within months; structured fields enforce the information quality required for retrieval and application.

rule.statement: Reject "communication was bad" and any entry where symptom equals cause. Push back with: "go one level deeper — what specifically broke, and why?"
rule.rationale: Vague entries produce vague recommendations; "improve communication" cannot be actioned or measured. A specific mechanism and fix can.

rule.statement: Quantify impact in dollars, days, defects, or incidents. "Saved time" is not a lesson; "avoided 12 dev-days of rework" is.
rule.rationale: Quantified impact enables prioritization: a lesson that saved 12 dev-days should be in the kickoff checklist; one that saved 30 minutes should not be.

rule.statement: Capture lessons at each of these triggers, not only at project close-out: after each milestone, when a problem occurs, after a significant success, during retrospectives, at vendor handoffs, and at project closure.
rule.rationale: End-of-project capture misses 60-80% of lessons; details are forgotten and the team has moved on. Continuous capture is the only way to preserve context.

example.note: Technical lesson: chose a new framework without prior team experience. Impact: development took 40% longer (est. 12 dev-days lost). Root cause: excitement about technology + underestimated training time (5-why: why did scope of training not appear in estimates? — because new tech was not flagged as a risk during kickoff). Lesson: new technology adoption requires explicit training time in the WBS. Recommendation: when introducing unfamiliar tech, add 25% buffer to estimates + schedule dedicated training. Owner: PM. Where applied: planning checklist.

section.title: Session Ground Rules

rule.statement: Run lessons-learned sessions blameless: focus on systems and processes, not individuals. Apply 5-whys and reject "human error" as a terminal cause.
rule.rationale: Blame culture turns sessions into theater; people stop sharing real failures. Blameless sessions produce actionable root causes that can change the system rather than the person.

section.p: Session structure (75 min): opening + ground rules 5min, what worked 15min, what did not work 15min, root causes 15min, recommendations 15min, action items with owners 10min.
